blockingIssues.description: The public study API exports `createStudy`, `updateStudy`, `launchStudy`, and `cancelStudy`, but nothing that moves a freshly-created draft into `persona_review` or `ready`. `launchStudy` then hard-rejects draft studies, so a study created through the supported public surface can never become launchable without tests or internal mutations directly patching state. That leaves the 10-state machine unreachable from real callers and misses the feature contract's promised ready-state validation step.

When accepted persona variants are missing, `launchStudy` only patches the study back to `persona_review` and returns. There is no call into the persona-variant generation action or any queued follow-up work, so VAL-STUDY-014's required “transition to persona_review and trigger variant generation” behavior never happens. A ready study with no variants therefore stalls permanently until something external seeds variants by hand.

After marking a stale run `infra_error`, the monitor calls `internal.waveDispatch.dispatchStudyWave`. That helper immediately returns unless the study status is `queued` or `running` (`convex/waveDispatch.ts:87`), so a stale run that occurs while the study is `replaying` frees capacity without dispatching queued replay replacements. The feature contract says stale runs should release the slot and let the next queued run dispatch; in replay mode the current code leaves the study stranded with undispatched queued runs.

`completeStudyLifecycleAfterReplay` unconditionally transitions every fully-settled replaying study to `analyzing` and then `completed` after creating a report. There is no guard for the “all runs ended `infra_error`” path, so a study whose initial and replay runs all fail for infrastructure reasons still completes with a report instead of transitioning to `failed` as required by VAL-STUDY-065. The workflow therefore misclassifies platform outages as completed studies.

This route module only calls `api.studies.createStudy`, and the wizard submit action is explicitly “Save study draft” (`apps/web/src/routes/study-pages.tsx:553`). There is no UI that invokes `api.studies.updateStudy` or `api.studies.launchStudy`, so a researcher can create a study but cannot advance or launch it from the browser. That leaves the milestone's create → configure → launch flow incomplete on the user-visible surface.

The live-monitor card only renders read-only metrics plus an “Open runs tab” link (`apps/web/src/routes/study-pages.tsx:910`). The feature never imports or calls `api.studies.cancelStudy`, so there is no cancellation button or confirmation flow anywhere on the monitoring surface despite the feature description promising cancellation actions. That blocks the required mid-execution cancel flow from the user-visible UI.
